comment_text.ud3.7:3.6: I’m not sure if anekarasabyañjano is correct, although the word does occur at Pv 13:15.4 and Pv 27:12.4. The commentary, while not definitive, suggests anekasūpabyañjano (anekehi sūpehi ceva byañjanehi ca), which would be more idiomatic. However it is not attested.

root_text.ud3.7:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud3.7:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā mahākassapo pippaliguhāyaṁ viharati, sattāhaṁ ekapallaṅkena nisinno hoti aññataraṁ samādhiṁ samāpajjitvā.
root_text.ud3.7:1.4: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo tassa sattāhassa accayena tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhāsi.
root_text.ud3.7:1.5: Atha kho āyasmato mahākassapassa tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhitassa etadahosi:
root_text.ud3.7:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena pañcamattāni devatāsatāni ussukkaṁ āpannāni honti āyasmato mahākassapassa piṇḍapātapaṭilābhāya.
root_text.ud3.7:2.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo tāni pañcamattāni devatāsatāni paṭikkhipitvā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.ud3.7:2.3: Tena kho pana samayena sakko devānamindo āyasmato mahākassapassa piṇḍapātaṁ dātukāmo hoti.
root_text.ud3.7:3.2: Pesakāravaṇṇaṁ abhinimminitvā tantaṁ vināti. Sujā asurakaññā tasaraṁ pūreti.
root_text.ud3.7:3.3: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo rājagahe sapadānaṁ piṇḍāya caramāno yena sakkassa devānamindassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.ud3.7:3.4: Addasā kho sakko devānamindo āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.ud3.7:3.5: Disvāna gharā nikkhamitvā paccuggantvā hatthato pattaṁ gahetvā gharaṁ pavisitvā ghaṭiyā odanaṁ uddharitvā pattaṁ pūretvā āyasmato mahākassapassa adāsi.
root_text.ud3.7:3.6: So ahosi piṇḍapāto anekasūpo anekabyañjano anekarasabyañjano.
root_text.ud3.7:3.8: “ko nu kho ayaṁ satto yassāyaṁ evarūpo iddhānubhāvo”ti?
root_text.ud3.7:4.1: Atha kho sakko devānamindo āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā vehāsaṁ abbhuggantvā ākāse antalikkhe tikkhattuṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud3.7:4.5: Assosi kho bhagavā dibbāya sotadhātuyā visuddhāya atikkantamānusikāya sakkassa devānamindassa vehāsaṁ abbhuggantvā ākāse antalikkhe tikkhattuṁ udānaṁ udānentassa:
root_text.ud3.7:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud3.7:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud3.7:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Mahākassapa was staying in the Pipphali cave. Having entered a certain state of immersion, he sat cross-legged for seven days without moving.
translation_text.ud3.7:1.4: When seven days had passed, Mahākassapa emerged from that state of immersion.
translation_text.ud3.7:2.1: Now at that time five hundred deities were ready and eager for the chance to offer alms to Mahākassapa.
translation_text.ud3.7:2.2: But Mahākasspa turned down those deities. In the morning, he robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.ud3.7:2.3: Now at that time Sakka, lord of gods, wished to give alms to Mahākassapa.
translation_text.ud3.7:3.2: Having manifested in the appearance of an embroiderer, he worked the loom while the titan maiden Sujā fed the shuttle.
translation_text.ud3.7:3.3: Then, as Mahākassapa wandered indiscriminately for almsfood in Rājagaha, he approached Sakka’s house.
translation_text.ud3.7:3.4: Seeing Mahākassapa coming off in the distance,
translation_text.ud3.7:3.5: Sakka came out of his house, greeted him, and took the bowl from his hand. He re-entered the house and filled the bowl with rice from the pot.
translation_text.ud3.7:3.6: That almsfood had many tasty soups and sauces.
translation_text.ud3.7:3.8: “Now, what being is this who has such psychic power?”
translation_text.ud3.7:3.11: Knowing this, he said to Sakka,
translation_text.ud3.7:3.13: don’t do such a thing again.”
translation_text.ud3.7:3.14: “But Honorable Kassapa, we too need merit!
translation_text.ud3.7:3.15: We too ought to make merit.”
translation_text.ud3.7:4.1: Then Sakka bowed and respectfully circled Mahākassapa, keeping him on his right. Then he rose into the air and, sitting cross-legged in midair, expressed this heartfelt sentiment three times:
translation_text.ud3.7:4.2: “Oh the gift, the best gift is well established in Kassapa!
translation_text.ud3.7:4.3: Oh the gift, the best gift is well established in Kassapa!
translation_text.ud3.7:4.4: Oh the gift, the best gift is well established in Kassapa!”
translation_text.ud3.7:4.5: With clairaudience that is purified and superhuman, the Buddha heard Sakka express this heartfelt sentiment while sitting in the sky.
translation_text.ud3.7:5.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud3.7:6.3: the unaffected one is envied by even the gods,

variant_text.ud3.7:1.3: hoti aññataraṁ → nisinno aññataraṁ (sya-all, mr) | mā → māssu (bj)
